# ID-30: Roadmap-backlog consolidation + roadmap rethink (Linear-style)

Consolidate the current product-roadmap.json content into product-backlog.json (single canonical work list), then rethink the roadmap as a thin capability-themes surface per Linear / Aha! best practice.

Motivation: Per s48-feedback.md line 201 (verbatim: "items currently on the KH Roadmap (which is actually our Backlog - concepts reversed)") the current roadmap is misused as a parking lot for prioritised work, while the backlog is treated as deferred-only. This is the inverted semantic vs standard product management convention. FU-1 (label-reversal content migration) and FU-5 (file-rename completing reversal) were DEFERRED at S52 per surface-migration TECH §Follow-ups; the S60 ID-15.9 label-reversal-residue-audit returned CLEAN but applied triage-finding Branch B target-semantic (strategic/tactical) — not the user-intent Linear semantic.

Approach (per S62 W2 Liam ratification): (1) audit roadmap vs backlog for duplication; (2) remove items rendered redundant by canonical-pipeline implementation (collapse list); (3) move remaining roadmap items → backlog (post-collapse); (4) rethink roadmap to industry best practice (Shape A: ~10-12 capability themes with now/next/later horizons); (5) audit consolidated backlog for further dedup against task-list + propose prioritisation model with optional rank field; (6) extend task-view (ID-20) backlog CRUD model to support priority/rank editing.

## Acceptance criteria

- product-backlog.json grows by ~54 items per RESEARCH §3 migration proposal (post-Liam OQ ratification).
- product-roadmap.json reshaped to Shape A (or alternative per OQ-1) with ~10-12 capability themes.
- triage-finding + update-roadmap-backlog skills rewritten under new semantic.
- Optional rank field added to BacklogSchema; backlog test guards updated.
- task-view (ID-20) ID-20.14 extension surfaces backlog edit + drag-reorder per ID-20.14 PLAN authored in TECH phase.
- Migration sequencing executes in 3 PRs (schema/skill update → bulk content migration → roadmap reshape) per OQ-7.

## Dependencies

ID-15 done (schema baseline). Independent of ID-9 (Astro docs) + ID-20 (task-view) at RESEARCH level; PRODUCT/TECH execution may interlock with ID-20.14 backlog-edit surface.

DONE-S68-W2 — All 13 Subtasks done (PR-A + PR-B + PR-C shipped). 11 capability themes authored; sections[] dropped; schema reshape -144 LOC; 54 net-new backlog entries IDs 87-140; 8 REMOVE_REDUNDANT roadmap items removed; renderer rewritten for theme-traversal; capability_theme back-filled on 21 Tasks. End-of-task simplification + quality-review SKIPPED per S67 W1c precedent (per-Subtask Checker dispatches × 9 already verified; code surface = schema + scripts + tests + data + ledger writes, no behavior-critical logic warranting separate axes).

All purely additive against live JSON content.

**Details:**

Three additive Zod schema changes per TECH §3.1:

lib/validation/backlog-schema.ts (~line 122, between notes and details):
```typescript
/**
 * Within-priority deterministic ordering. Lower integer = higher rank.
 * Default null; pre-existing items omit. Schema does NOT enforce uniqueness
 * or contiguity within tier (PRODUCT inv 3). Curator skill maintains
 * discipline (Subtask 30.5 + P-OQ-3 auto-shift default).
 */
rank: z.number().int().nullable().optional(),
```

lib/validation/task-list-schema.ts (~line 130, after array-fields block, before .strict()):
```typescript
/**
 * Optional back-link to a Roadmap theme (OQ-6 ratification). Authoritative
 * direction is theme.linked_tasks[]; capability_theme is convenience back-link
 * the curator skill maintains in sync. Absent = unaffiliated.
 */
capability_theme: z.string().nullable().optional(),
```

lib/validation/roadmap-schema.ts:
- Add RoadmapThemeSchema export per TECH §3.1 (10 Phase-B fields; .strict()):
  - id (BARE_ID_REGEX), title, description, time_horizon enum 3-value (now/next/later), status enum 3-value (pending/in_progress/done per P-OQ-1 default), linked_tasks string[], linked_backlog string[], session_refs string[], commit_refs string[], cross_doc_links DocLinkSchema[], notes string nullable.
- Reshape RoadmapSchema root: sections[] becomes OPTIONAL; add themes[] OPTIONAL; add .superRefine() enforcing exactly-one-of:
```typescript
.superRefine((doc, ctx) => {
  const hasSections = doc.sections !== undefined;
  const hasThemes = doc.themes !== undefined;
  if (hasSections === hasThemes) {
    ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', path: ['sections', 'themes'],
      message: 'Exactly one of sections or themes must be present.' });
  }
});
```
Per T-OQ-4 ratification: stay with superRefine (not discriminatedUnion) to avoid discriminator-field content churn.

lib/backlog/sort.ts (NEW pure helper, verifies inv 4):
```typescript
export function sortBacklogItems(items: BacklogItem[]): BacklogItem[] {
  const priorityOrder: Record<Priority, number> = {
    must: 0, should: 1, could: 2, future: 3,
    high: 4, medium: 5, low: 6, trigger: 7,
  };
  return [...items].sort((a, b) => {
    const pDelta = priorityOrder[a.priority] - priorityOrder[b.priority];
    if (pDelta !== 0) return pDelta;
    const rA = a.rank ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER;
    const rB = b.rank ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER;
    if (rA !== rB) return rA - rB;
    return parseInt(a.id, 10) - parseInt(b.id, 10);
  });
}
```

__tests__/lib/backlog/sort.test.ts (NEW; 8-case fixture covering priority changes, rank null vs set, id tiebreaker on bare-digit comparison '9' before '10' via parseInt).

parseRoadmapWithWarnings appended to lib/validation/roadmap-schema.ts (mirrors parseTaskListWithWarnings):
```typescript
export interface RoadmapWarning {
  themeCount?: number;
  message: string;
}

export function parseRoadmapWithWarnings(input: unknown): {
  value: Roadmap;
  warnings: RoadmapWarning[];
} {
  const value = RoadmapSchema.parse(input);
  const warnings: RoadmapWarning[] = [];
  if (value.themes && value.themes.length > 12) {
    warnings.push({ themeCount: value.themes.length,
      message: `Roadmap has ${value.themes.length} themes (>12). Per PRODUCT inv 8, consider merging.` });
  }
  return { value, warnings };
}
```

- lib/validation/roadmap-schema.ts (helper function append)

**T-OQ-1 PRODUCT.md amendment diff:**
```
inv 1: 'Today's 55 items grow to 109' → 'Today's 54 items grow to 108'
inv 12 PR-B acceptance gate: 'Backlog item count goes from 55 to 109' → 'Backlog item count goes from 54 to 108'
```
Both amendments carry the reconciliation parenthetical: 'T-OQ-1 reconciled at S67 W1a per Subtask 30.9 — original "55 → 109" reflected a stale count; live baseline measured 54'. Other 55/61 references on lines 16, 80 (roadmap composition factual claim) left unchanged per surgical-amendment scope.

**Design decisions worth flagging:**
1. **Idempotency vs collision boundary.** PLAN.md test (c) says 'one id pre-populated; script reports collision and does NOT write' but PRODUCT inv 15 says idempotent re-runs are no-ops. The reconciliation: classifyFixtureEntry() returns 'absent' / 'migration-skip' / 'collision' based on BOTH id-match AND presence of the canonical 'Migrated from roadmap §' marker in the existing entry's notes. An existing entry with matching id + marker → silent skip (idempotent). An existing entry with matching id + no marker → collision (independently authored entry shadows this id; abort before write). This is the only behaviour consistent with both spec lines.
2. **Synthetic pre-migration baseline in tests.** The live product-backlog.json HEAD has IDs 78-85 populated by independent triage-routed entries (not migration entries). A naive 'copy live JSON into tmpdir' baseline would trigger COLLISION on test (a) before any migration logic runs. Tests filter the live JSON to drop any IDs 78-131, producing a clean pre-migration baseline that mirrors the PR-B starting state PRODUCT inv 12 specifies. This isolates the script's migration behaviour from accidental coupling to the post-spec live data state.
3. **'partial 11.2' interpretation.** The brief / RESEARCH §2.3 line 245 cites '11.2 partially (rename-gated test partitions)'. The collapse-list summary §2.3 lines 238-245 lists 11.2 in the REMOVE_REDUNDANT 8-item set anyway; PRODUCT inv 12 PR-B clause explicitly enumerates '8 REMOVE_REDUNDANT items (3.2, 3.4, 3.6, 7.2, 7.6, 8.1, 11.17, and the rename-gated 11.2 partition)'. Operationally 11.2 is removed entirely (the surviving W-RE/W-RH scope migrates to other Backlog items, not preserved as a residual 11.2 entry). REMOVE_REDUNDANT_ROADMAP_IDS = ['3.2', '3.4', '3.6', '7.2', '7.6', '8.1', '11.17', '11.2'] — 8 ids — matches both the brief's enumeration and the 61→53 expected count delta.
4. **CLI gating via `import.meta.main`.** The script's main() runs only when invoked directly (`bun scripts/migrate-backlog-from-roadmap.ts`); tests import the module without triggering CLI side effects. Same pattern as roadmap-from-json.ts (`if (import.meta.main) { ... }`).
5. **Result-type contract instead of thrown errors.** `runMigration()` returns `MigrationResult = {ok: true, ...} | {ok: false, reason, detail}`. Only readJson() can propagate exceptions (on malformed JSON parse), which matches the project's tryQuery/Result-style convention from lib/supabase/safe — except here the resource is filesystem JSON not Supabase.

**Out-of-scope observations (Curator triage candidates):**
1. **Live backlog IDs 78-85 are independently-authored entries (NOT migration entries).** Per `jq '.items[] | select(.id == "78" or ... "85") | .notes'` against live HEAD, these were added by S64+ triage routing (S252, S64 W1, S65 Wave 2, S66 Wave 2 routes — see Backlog entry notes for provenance). Subtask 30.10 (live execution, Wave 2, gated on Liam ratification) will fail with COLLISION against these IDs unless one of: (a) the existing entries get renumbered to IDs outside 78-131; (b) the migration fixture IDs get shifted to start above the max live id (currently 85); (c) the existing entries get marked with the migration marker (semantically wrong — they're not migration entries). Option (a) seems cleanest — renumber existing 78-85 to ≥132 — but requires triage-finding routing decisions. Routing to Curator: this is exactly the kind of cross-Subtask reconciliation the Curator owns via update-roadmap-backlog Update mode + planning ahead of Subtask 30.10 execution. Surfaced as fyi; not load-bearing for 30.9 itself (the script + tests are correct; 30.10 needs a pre-execution decision).
2. **No similar discovery for IDs 86+.** A scan via `jq '.items | map(.id) | sort_by(tonumber)' docs/reference/product-backlog.json` confirms the live backlog uses IDs 17-85 only — IDs 86-131 are unused and the migration will not collide on those. Only the 78-85 range needs reconciliation.
3. **PARTIAL_OVERLAP cluster member ID-100 not present in pre-migration baseline.** Cluster 'ingest-UI' references IDs 61+62+79+100. After migration, ID-100 lives in the migrated set (it's in the RESEARCH §3.2 payload). The script handles this correctly via `indexById.get(memberId)` (skips missing members in the pre-migration state), and after the merge the lookup succeeds. Verified by test (g): all 4 cluster members carry the adjacency line post-migration. Surfacing this not as a bug but as confirmation that the across-pools cluster-walking design works as intended.
4. **Skill-tool-blocked discipline observed.** The Tier 2.2 hook correctly fired on my initial PRODUCT.md Edit when I used the absolute knowledge-hub-production-readiness sibling-tree path from CWD pointed at the agent worktree (`agent-a5c80852324ad348d` lives under `/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub/.claude/worktrees/`). I corrected to relative paths immediately. No leakage; hook working as designed (CLAUDE.md project-instruction worktree gotcha).

**Deviations from brief:**
- The brief said '1-line T-OQ-1 amendment'. The amendment is actually 2 line edits (inv 1 + inv 12 PR-B clause) — both 'load-bearing' references per the brief's own wording ('PRODUCT inv 1 + PR-B acceptance-gate text'). Each individual edit replaces one line in place; total is 2 surgical line edits. Reads as plural amendments rather than literally one line.
- The brief implied test (c) might be satisfied by a fixture with internal duplicates. I implemented test (c) per PLAN §2.2 'Mock a fixture with one id deliberately pre-populated' (i.e. pre-populated in the BACKLOG, not internal to the fixture). The fixture-internal-duplicate case is covered as an additional defensive test (`runMigration — additional defensive cases > rejects fixture carrying internal duplicate ids`). Both cases produce abort-without-write; the test coverage is strictly broader than the brief's 8 (a-h) line items.

### ID-30.11: Author 10-12 capability themes — populate themes[] array

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-30.10
- **Updated:** _unset_

Curator-authored content reshape replacing the 53-item / 16-section roadmap structure with 10-12 capability themes per PRODUCT inv 6, 7, 8. Each theme carries id, title, description (multi-paragraph Markdown), time_horizon (now/next/later), status (pending default per P-OQ-1), linked_tasks[] + linked_backlog[] (forward references), full Phase-B provenance.

**Test strategy:** All three PRODUCT inv 12 PR-C acceptance gates verified via jq: theme count ≤ 12 via jq '.themes | length'; orphan check via jq '.themes[] | select((.linked_tasks | length) == 0 and (.linked_backlog | length) == 0)' returns empty; time_horizon check via jq '.themes[] | select(.time_horizon == null)' returns empty.

**Details:**

Six-step authoring process per PRODUCT inv 6, 7, 8 + TECH §2 inv 6+7 rows:

1. Read post-PR-B docs/reference/product-roadmap.json (16 sections, 53 items).
2. Read post-PR-B docs/reference/product-backlog.json (108 items including 54 newly-migrated).
3. Read docs/reference/task-list.json (in-flight Tasks).
4. Identify ~10-12 capability themes covering the strategic surface. RESEARCH §4 named candidate themes (Sales Proposals, MCP server, ingestion-UI, taxonomy-and-vocab, intelligence-refinement, etc.) — re-evaluate against post-PR-B state.
5. For each theme: author id (bare-digit '1' onwards under Shape A), title (short UK English capability name), description (multi-paragraph Markdown — why this capability matters; outcome shape), time_horizon (now/next/later per OQ-2), status: 'pending' (P-OQ-1 default), linked_tasks[] (Task IDs contributing), linked_backlog[] (Backlog item IDs contributing), session_refs: ['kh-prod-readiness-S65'], commit_refs as relevant, cross_doc_links if relevant, notes free text.
6. Write document with new schema shape — drop sections[]; populate themes[]. RoadmapSchema union (from 30.6) accepts this in PR-C transitional state.

Theme cardinality discipline per PRODUCT inv 8: soft cap 12. If authoring surfaces 13th theme, consider merging per parseRoadmapWithWarnings warning (from 30.7). Re-evaluate quarterly.

PR-C acceptance gate per PRODUCT inv 12 (all three must hold):
- Theme count ≤ 12.
- Every theme has ≥ 1 linked_tasks[] OR linked_backlog[] entry (no orphan themes).
- Every theme has time_horizon set.

UK English discipline: every theme title + description in UK English (colour, organisation, behaviour, DD/MM/YYYY dates).

Coordination with Subtask 30.5 curator skill: update-roadmap-backlog Create-mode contract from 30.5 IS the contract used here. Author may invoke /update-roadmap-backlog Create once per theme to populate themes[] — verifying rewritten skill's contract works in practice.

### ID-30.13: Renderer update + capability_theme back-fill via reverse-map script

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-30.12
- **Updated:** _unset_

Rewrite scripts/roadmap-from-json.ts to emit theme-headings MD output (replacing legacy section-traversal). Author scripts/backfill-capability-theme.ts reverse-mapping from theme.linked_tasks[] to set Task.capability_theme on task-list.json. Update __tests__/docs/roadmap-roundtrip.test.ts to themes shape + add render-twice-assert-equal idempotency probe.

**Test strategy:** bun run roadmap:render runs cleanly; render-twice-produces-identical-output test green; back-fill script reports non-empty count of Tasks updated; round-trip test green against new shape; bun run test __tests__/docs/ green.

**Details:**

Three new artefacts per TECH §2 inv 6 + inv 9 + §3.2 order 8 + §7 risk row 9:

Renderer rewrite scripts/roadmap-from-json.ts:
- Replace section-traversal logic with theme-traversal.
- Emit one MD heading per theme:

```markdown
# {document_name}

## Theme: {title} ({time_horizon} — {status})

{description}

**Linked Tasks:** {linked_tasks.map(id => `[ID-${id}](...)`)}
**Linked Backlog:** {linked_backlog.map(id => `[BID-${id}](...)`)}
```

Idempotency probe (TECH §7 risk row 9): render-twice-assert-equal test added to round-trip suite. Output JSON ordering must be deterministic.

Back-fill script scripts/backfill-capability-theme.ts:

```typescript
import { RoadmapSchema } from '@/lib/validation/roadmap-schema';
import { TaskListSchema } from '@/lib/validation/task-list-schema';
import fs from 'fs';

const roadmap = RoadmapSchema.parse(JSON.parse(
  fs.readFileSync('docs/reference/product-roadmap.json', 'utf-8')));
const taskList = TaskListSchema.parse(JSON.parse(
  fs.readFileSync('docs/reference/task-list.json', 'utf-8')));

const reverseMap = new Map<string, string>();
for (const theme of roadmap.themes ?? []) {
  for (const taskId of theme.linked_tasks) {
    if (reverseMap.has(taskId)) {
      console.warn(`Task ${taskId} linked from multiple themes — leaving capability_theme unset`);
      reverseMap.set(taskId, ''); // sentinel for ambiguous
    } else {
      reverseMap.set(taskId, theme.id);
    }
  }
}

let updatedCount = 0;
for (const task of taskList.tasks) {
  const themeId = reverseMap.get(task.id);
  if (themeId && themeId !== '') {
    task.capability_theme = themeId;
    updatedCount++;
  }
}

fs.writeFileSync('docs/reference/task-list.json',
  JSON.stringify(taskList, null, 2) + '\n');
console.log(`Back-filled capability_theme on ${updatedCount} Tasks`);
```

T-OQ-3 cardinality check (RATIFIED): if >30 Tasks affected, escalate to Orchestrator at script-run time (split into 30.13a back-fill + 30.13b renderer). With ~36 Tasks in task-list.json today, likely 10-20 affected — under 30-Task threshold; no split needed.

Round-trip test update __tests__/docs/roadmap-roundtrip.test.ts:
- Update fixture + assertions to themes shape.
- Add render-twice-assert-equal probe per TECH §7 risk row 9.

Determinism: renderer output JSON ordering must be deterministic so round-trip test is idempotent.

Touch zone:
- scripts/roadmap-from-json.ts (rewrite)
- scripts/backfill-capability-theme.ts (NEW)
- docs/reference/task-list.json (back-fill via script)
- __tests__/docs/roadmap-roundtrip.test.ts (update)

Spec references: PRODUCT.md inv 6, 9, 12 PR-C clause; TECH.md §2 inv 6+9 + §3.2 order 8 + §7 risk row 9; PLAN.md §2.3 Subtask 30.13.

**FORWARD-NOTE (S66 W1 Checker F-2 — curator a45a674ab6204965b, HEAD 18b66c7c):** The current `scripts/roadmap-from-json.ts` iterates `roadmap.sections` at lines 288, 347, and 349 without a null guard. ID-30.6 made `RoadmapSchema.sections` optional (`RoadmapSection[] | undefined`), opening a latent `TypeError` if a themes-only document is passed to the renderer before this subtask ships. The script is excluded from tsconfig compilation so tsc does not surface this. The rewrite in this subtask naturally eliminates the risk (sections-traversal code is replaced entirely), but the executor MUST NOT assume the old traversal code is safe to leave in place during any partial-rewrite state. Specifically: if the rewrite proceeds incrementally, ensure `roadmap.sections ?? []` guards are applied at lines 288, 347, and 349 of the PRE-rewrite file before any intermediate commit that could be run against a themes-only document. The safest approach is an atomic replacement of all section-traversal logic in a single commit.

**S68 W2 SCOPE EXPANSION (Option β ratified, S68 AskUserQuestion post 30.12 executor escalation a8dd0e6a18356b9c3):** 30.13 also owns: (a) `scripts/roadmap-to-json.ts` rewrite for theme-shape (symmetric pair to roadmap-from-json.ts; was missing from original PLAN.md §2.3 list — references removed RoadmapSection / RoadmapItem types at lines 52-53, 428, 443, 453, 484, 487, 580, 609, 612; also excluded from tsconfig so tsc does not surface, but runtime-broken on themes-only docs); (b) `scripts/migrate-backlog-from-roadmap.ts:421-433` dead branch cleanup (`if (roadmap.sections) { ... }` → drop entirely; script is 30.10-historic, branch unreachable in live data after 30.11 themes[] population); (c) reframe `__tests__/scripts/migrate-backlog-from-roadmap.test.ts > test (h)` (line 487) post-30.12 RoadmapSchema rejection of sections-shape sandbox (options: `it.skip` with 30.10-historic comment, OR re-seed sandbox with themes-shape + assert idempotent no-op — `git show 840f140a:docs/reference/product-roadmap.json` returns pre-migration sections-shape which post-30.12 schema rejects; migration suite reports 15/16 PASS until reframed). All three were noted as expected-red in Subtask 30.12 commit message + journal block under Option β.

What shipped: Subtask 30.13 (Renderer + capability_theme back-fill + Option β scope expansion) — the closing Subtask of Task ID-30 PR-C Wave 2. Three new artefacts + two file cleanups + two test updates:

1. `scripts/roadmap-from-json.ts` — full atomic rewrite for the themes-shape `RoadmapSchema` landed in 30.12. Replaced section-traversal with theme-traversal emitting one heading-per-theme MD. Determinism: themes iterate via for-loop (no `Object.keys()` / map-iteration), no timestamps, no UUIDs. Render-twice-assert-equal idempotency probe holds.

2. `scripts/backfill-capability-theme.ts` — NEW. Reverse-maps `theme.linked_tasks[]` → `Task.capability_theme`. Built a `Map<task_id, theme_id>`; Tasks linked from multiple themes are flagged as ambiguous and left for the curator to resolve explicitly (per P-OQ-4 default). T-OQ-3 cardinality threshold 30 Tasks; observed 21 Tasks affected — well under threshold; no split required. Script is idempotent: re-runs produce identical task-list.json.

Symmetric pair to roadmap-from-json.ts: parses themes-shape MD back to JSON via `THEME_HEADING_RE` / `STATUS_LINE_RE` / `LINKED_TASKS_RE` + cross-doc-link-bullet parser.
